description: Native Sybil AI market catalog (rev 4, 2026-07-15), owned by sybil-native. Grouped children carry explicit natural-language market titles in addition to their parent event titles. Duplicate legacy contracts are consolidated; the distinct final-week OpenRouter event is retained; source-event artwork is self-hosted from reviewed brand assets.

markets.title: Which company will lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Anthropic
outcomes.market_title: Will Anthropic lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: OpenAI
outcomes.market_title: Will OpenAI lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Google
outcomes.market_title: Will Google lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: xAI
outcomes.market_title: Will xAI lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company lead the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the company whose model holds the #1 position (highest Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index score) on the Artificial Analysis model leaderboard (https://artificialanalysis.ai/leaderboards/models), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. Model-to-company mapping: any Claude model = Anthropic, any GPT or o-series model = OpenAI, any Gemini model = Google, any Grok model = xAI, any DeepSeek model = DeepSeek. If the top model's company is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: on identical Index scores, the model listed higher on the leaderboard wins. If the Index is renamed or its methodology re-versioned, use the successor headline intelligence ranking on the same page. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use the Artificial Analysis models API (https://artificialanalysis.ai/api/v2/data/llms/models), taking the model with the highest Intelligence Index and reading its creator field. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://artificialanalysis.ai/leaderboards/models taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: The API is machine-readable JSON but needs a free API key; unauthenticated requests return HTTP 401. Because the key may not be obtainable at read time, the rendered leaderboard is the primary source and the API is the fallback.

outcomes.market_title: Will Xiaomi rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: MiniMax
outcomes.market_title: Will MiniMax rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: Z AI (GLM)
outcomes.market_title: Will Z AI (GLM) rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: Anthropic
outcomes.market_title: Will Anthropic rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: Google
outcomes.market_title: Will Google rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: OpenAI
outcomes.market_title: Will OpenAI rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company rank #1 by token usage on OpenRouter for the final week of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the authoring organization of the model with the highest total token usage on OpenRouter over the seven UTC days 2026-12-25 through 2026-12-31 inclusive, summed across those days per OpenRouter's daily rankings dataset. If the top organization is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: the higher single-day peak inside the window. If the daily rankings dataset cannot be read at that time, use the public weekly rankings page (https://openrouter.ai/rankings) for the week ending 2026-12-31; if that week is not published, the last complete week of 2026 shown there. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://openrouter.ai/rankings taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Dataset endpoint: https://openrouter.ai/docs/api/api-reference/datasets/get-rankings-daily (GET). Requires the OpenRouter API key already held for the arena bots. Sum per-model token usage over 2026-12-25..2026-12-31 UTC, then map the winning model to its authoring organization.

markets.title: Which company leads OpenRouter LLM usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Google
outcomes.market_title: Will Google lead OpenRouter text-LLM token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: DeepSeek
outcomes.market_title: Will DeepSeek lead OpenRouter text-LLM token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Anthropic
outcomes.market_title: Will Anthropic lead OpenRouter text-LLM token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: OpenAI
outcomes.market_title: Will OpenAI lead OpenRouter text-LLM token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company lead OpenRouter text-LLM token usage in December 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the authoring organization with the largest share of text-LLM token volume on OpenRouter over December 2026, per the 'Market Share' (share by model author) view at https://openrouter.ai/rankings, read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC with the window set to the calendar month December 2026 (2026-12-01..2026-12-31 UTC). If no calendar-month filter is offered, use the trailing 30-day 'Month' view. Models map to their author: any Gemini = Google, any GPT or o-series = OpenAI, any Claude = Anthropic, any DeepSeek model = DeepSeek, any Qwen = Alibaba, any Grok = xAI, any MiniMax model = MiniMax. If the leading author is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: the company with the higher absolute count over the window; if still tied, the alphabetically first name. If OpenRouter renames or restructures the page, use the closest successor view of the same measure. If the page cannot be read at that time, use the OpenRouter rankings dataset API for the same window, aggregated by model author. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://openrouter.ai/rankings taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Open OpenRouter LLM Rankings, select the 'Market Share' tab (share by model author), set the range to December 2026, and record the author with the highest token share. The dataset API fallback requires an OpenRouter API key.

markets.title: Which app leads OpenRouter usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Kilo Code

outcomes.market_title: Will Hermes lead OpenRouter app token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Roo Code
outcomes.market_title: Will Roo Code lead OpenRouter app token usage in December 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another app lead OpenRouter app token usage in December 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the app with the largest total token usage (prompt + completion) on OpenRouter over December 2026, per the App & Agent Rankings, sorted by total token volume at https://openrouter.ai/apps, read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC with the window set to the calendar month December 2026 (2026-12-01..2026-12-31 UTC). If no calendar-month filter is offered, use the trailing 30-day 'Month' view. Apps are identified by their canonical OpenRouter app name; traffic from related aliases is merged into the canonical visible app, and hidden or private apps are excluded. If the leading app is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: the app with the higher absolute count over the window; if still tied, the alphabetically first name. If OpenRouter renames or restructures the page, use the closest successor view of the same measure. If the page cannot be read at that time, use the OpenRouter rankings dataset API for the same window, aggregated by app. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://openrouter.ai/apps taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Open OpenRouter App & Agent Rankings sorted by 'popular' (total token volume), set the range to December 2026, and record the top-ranked app. The dataset API fallback requires an OpenRouter API key.

outcomes.market_title: Will xAI have the top GPQA Diamond score at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company have the top GPQA Diamond score at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the company whose model holds the highest score on the LLM-Stats GPQA Diamond leaderboard (https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/gpqa-diamond), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. Model-to-company mapping: any Claude model = Anthropic, any GPT or o-series model = OpenAI, any Gemini model = Google, any Grok model = xAI, any DeepSeek model = DeepSeek, any Qwen model = Alibaba. If the top model's company is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: on identical scores, the company of the model listed higher on the page wins. If LLM-Stats renames or re-versions the benchmark, use its closest successor on the same host. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use the Artificial Analysis GPQA Diamond evaluation (https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/gpqa-diamond). If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/gpqa-diamond taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Open https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/gpqa-diamond, sort by score descending, and record the company of the single highest-scoring model using the mapping in the description.

markets.title: What will the top SWE-bench Verified score be at end of 2026?

outcomes.market_title: Will the top SWE-bench Verified score be <93% at end of 2026?
Will the top SWE-bench Verified score be between 93% and 95% at end of 2026?
Will the top SWE-bench Verified score be between 95% and 97% at end of 2026?
Will the top SWE-bench Verified score be between 97% and 99% at end of 2026?
Will the top SWE-bench Verified score be ≥99% at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the bucket containing the single highest score on the LLM-Stats SWE-bench Verified leaderboard (https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/swe-bench-verified), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. The score is read as a percentage (0-100%); a 0-1 decimal is multiplied by 100. Only the single highest score across all listed models counts, whichever company holds it. Buckets are lower-bound inclusive and upper-bound exclusive, so a value exactly on a boundary falls in the higher bucket (95.0% falls in '95-97%'). If LLM-Stats renames or re-versions the benchmark, use its closest successor on the same host. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use the Artificial Analysis SWE-bench Verified evaluation (https://artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/swe-bench-verified). If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/swe-bench-verified taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Open https://llm-stats.com/benchmarks/swe-bench-verified, take the highest score across all models, convert to a percentage, and select the bucket.

markets.title: Which company's model will have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Anthropic
outcomes.market_title: Will Anthropic have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: OpenAI
outcomes.market_title: Will OpenAI have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Google
outcomes.market_title: Will Google have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: xAI
outcomes.market_title: Will xAI have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company have the longest METR 50% time horizon at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the company whose best model has the longest measured 50%-task-completion time horizon on the METR Time Horizon 1.1 leaderboard (https://metr.org/time-horizons/), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. Model-to-company mapping: any Claude model = Anthropic, any GPT or o-series model = OpenAI, any Gemini model = Google, any Grok model = xAI. If the leading model's company is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: the higher point estimate wins, including when the gap is within measurement noise; on an exact numeric tie, the model with the earlier METR publication date. If METR versions the methodology (e.g. 1.2), read the successor 50% leaderboard. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use Epoch AI's mirror of the same measurement (https://epoch.ai/benchmarks/metr-time-horizons). If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://metr.org/time-horizons/ taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: The chart renders client-side; wait for it to populate before reading. Select the 50% time-horizon view and take the single highest point estimate.

markets.resolution_criteria: Each outcome resolves YES if the longest 50%-task-completion time horizon of any model on the METR Time Horizon 1.1 leaderboard (https://metr.org/time-horizons/) is at least its hour threshold, as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. The value is the maximum 50%-horizon point estimate across all listed models, whichever company holds it. A value exactly equal to a threshold meets it. METR notes that measurements above 16 hours are unreliable with the current task suite; the published point estimate is still used as-is. If METR versions the methodology (e.g. 1.2), read the successor 50% leaderboard. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use Epoch AI's mirror of the same measurement (https://epoch.ai/benchmarks/metr-time-horizons). If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://metr.org/time-horizons/ taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: The chart renders client-side; wait for it to populate before reading. Select the 50% time-horizon view, take the maximum point estimate across all plotted models, convert to hours, and mark each rung YES whose threshold is at or below that value.

markets.title: How large will the biggest public-API model context window be at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: ≥2M tokens
outcomes.market_title: Will the largest advertised public-API context window be ≥2M tokens at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: ≥3M tokens
outcomes.market_title: Will the largest advertised public-API context window be ≥3M tokens at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: ≥5M tokens
outcomes.market_title: Will the largest advertised public-API context window be ≥5M tokens at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Each outcome resolves YES if, at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC, the largest maximum context window advertised for a generally-available model on a standard public API is at least its token threshold. 'Generally available on a standard public API' excludes downloadable open-weights and self-hosted-only models, research previews, waitlist or private-beta access, and enterprise-gated tiers that are not on the standard API. 'Advertised' means the officially listed maximum input context length, counting the headline figure even where effective usable context is lower. A value exactly equal to a threshold meets it. The binding source is the provider's own official API documentation, checked across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, DeepSeek and any newer frontier entrant. If a provider's own documentation cannot be read at that time, use the WhatLLM largest-context tracker (https://whatllm.org/largest-context-window-llm), which scopes to public-API frontier models. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://whatllm.org/largest-context-window-llm taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Check the official API docs for each listed provider, exclude ineligible model classes, then cross-check the largest figure against the WhatLLM tracker.

markets.resolution_criteria: Each outcome resolves YES if Anthropic's flagship model — the highest-numbered non-deprecated Opus model — has a standard base API input price at or below its dollar threshold per million input tokens, as read on Anthropic's official pricing page (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing) at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. Use the standard global, non-cached, non-batch input price. A price exactly equal to a threshold counts as at or below. If several flagship-tier models are current, use the highest-numbered one. If the page is renamed or the flagship rebranded, use the successor official page and the equivalent flagship. If Anthropic retires the line without a priced successor, every outcome resolves NO. If the pricing page cannot be read at that time, use the same standard input price on https://www.anthropic.com/pricing. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Read the standard input $/MTok for the highest-numbered non-deprecated Opus model and compare against each rung.

markets.title: Which company will top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Anthropic
outcomes.market_title: Will Anthropic top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: OpenAI
outcomes.market_title: Will OpenAI top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Google
outcomes.market_title: Will Google top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: xAI
outcomes.market_title: Will xAI top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

outcomes.title: Other
outcomes.market_title: Will another company top KernelBench-Mega at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the company whose model holds the single highest recorded decode speedup on the KernelBench-Mega board (https://kernelbench.com/mega), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. The measure is the decode speedup over the optimized-PyTorch baseline (the `score` / peak_fraction column) on problem 02_kimi_linear_decode, across all models and GPUs. Only rows marked correct with a numeric score count; runs removed by the contamination tripwire do not. Mapping: any Claude model (Opus, Sonnet, Fable) = Anthropic; any GPT, o-series or Codex = OpenAI; any Gemini = Google; any Grok = xAI; any GLM = Z.ai; any DeepSeek = DeepSeek; any Kimi = Moonshot. If the leader's company is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: on scores equal to three decimals, the higher decode tok/s wins; if still tied, the earlier run_id timestamp. If the board is renamed or re-versioned, follow the successor decode-speedup board for the Kimi-Linear W4A16 decode megakernel. If the board cannot be read at that time, use the published results CSV (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Infatoshi/kernelbench.com/master/public/data/mega/results.csv). If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://kernelbench.com/mega taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Filter the CSV to problem == 02_kimi_linear_decode with correct == true and a numeric `score`; take the max per model, then the global max, and map that model to its company. If the CSV path 404s, the rendered board and https://github.com/Infatoshi/kernelbench.com/blob/master/benchmarks/mega/LEADERBOARD.md carry the same table.

outcomes.market_title: Will another company top OSWorld 2.0 at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the company whose model or agent ranks #1 by binary completion on the Snorkel AI OSWorld 2.0 leaderboard (https://snorkel.ai/leaderboard/os-world-2-0/), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. The measure is binary completion — strict end-to-end task success at the 500-step budget — not the partial-credit score. Mapping: any Claude model = Anthropic, any GPT or o-series model = OpenAI, any Gemini model = Google, any Qwen model = Alibaba, any Kimi model = Moonshot AI, any Seed or Doubao model = ByteDance. If the leader is not a listed outcome, resolves Other. Tie-break: on equal binary completion, the higher partial score ranks first; if still tied, the entry printed higher on the page. If the leaderboard is renamed or re-versioned, read the successor OSWorld 2.0 success-rate table at the same host. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use the OSWorld 2.0 results table in the Snorkel research paper (https://snorkel.ai/research-paper/os-world-2/), reading the same binary end-to-end success column — never an OSWorld-Verified or partial-credit score. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://snorkel.ai/leaderboard/os-world-2-0/ taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Find the row with the highest binary-completion percentage at the 500-step budget and map its model name to a company.

markets.title: What will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be at end of 2026?

outcomes.market_title: Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be <20% at end of 2026?
Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be between 20% and 25% at end of 2026?
Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be between 25% and 30% at end of 2026?
Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be between 30% and 40% at end of 2026?
Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be between 40% and 50% at end of 2026?
Will the top OSWorld 2.0 success rate be ≥50% at end of 2026?

markets.resolution_criteria: Resolves to the bucket containing the single highest binary completion score across all systems on the Snorkel AI OSWorld 2.0 leaderboard (https://snorkel.ai/leaderboard/os-world-2-0/), as read at 2026-12-31 23:59 UTC. The measure is binary completion — strict end-to-end task success at the 500-step budget — not the partial-credit score. The score is read as a percentage (0-100%); a 0-1 decimal is multiplied by 100. Only the single highest score across all listed models counts, whichever company holds it. Buckets are lower-bound inclusive and upper-bound exclusive, so a value exactly on a boundary falls in the higher bucket (25.0% falls in '25-30%'). If the leaderboard is renamed or re-versioned, read the successor OSWorld 2.0 binary-success table at the same host. If the leaderboard cannot be read at that time, use the OSWorld 2.0 results table in the Snorkel research paper (https://snorkel.ai/research-paper/os-world-2/), reading the same binary end-to-end success column — never an OSWorld-Verified or partial-credit score. If that is also unavailable, use the most recent Internet Archive (web.archive.org) capture of https://snorkel.ai/leaderboard/os-world-2-0/ taken on or before the read time. If none of those yields a reading, every outcome resolves NO.
markets.operator_notes: Read the binary-completion column at the 500-step budget and take the maximum across all rows.
